feat(nats): add deadline-based request timeouts and cleanup helpers

Introduce timeout-aware request handling for the NATS client:

- Add timeout_now() helper that reads current time from the Cx timer
  driver when available, falling back to wall_now() for injectable
  time source support in tests.
- Add read_more_until() method that wraps read_more() with a deadline,
  returning a TimedOut error when the deadline expires.
- Add cleanup_request_subscription() helper for consistent UNSUB
  cleanup with warning logging on failure.
- Refactor request() to use deadline-based timeout tracking instead
  of unbounded reads, ensuring requests fail promptly when the server
  doesn't respond within the configured timeout.
- Update .beads/issues.jsonl with latest tracker state.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
style: minor idiom improvements and clippy compliance

Small cleanup across several files, no behavioral changes:

- benches/tracing_overhead.rs: Replace || RuntimeState::new() closure
  with RuntimeState::new function pointer in iter_batched call.
- http_client.rs: Use const { Cell::new(0) } for thread_local static
  initialization (const-evaluable initializer).
- listener.rs: Same const Cell init pattern; also replace closure
  |t| t.get() with std::cell::Cell::get method reference.
- state.rs: Add #[allow(clippy::too_many_lines)] to
  advance_region_state which is intentionally a long iterative
  function (avoids recursion for stack depth control).
- connection.rs: Replace if-let-else with let-else pattern for
  cleaner early return on Poll::Pending.
- collect.rs: Add .clone() to expected in test assertion to satisfy
  move checker.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
refactor(lab): replace std HashSet with DetHashSet for deterministic scheduling

Switch LabScheduler.scheduled from std::collections::HashSet to
DetHashSet (deterministic hash set). The standard HashSet uses
randomized hashing which introduces non-determinism in iteration
order — problematic for a lab/testing scheduler whose entire purpose
is deterministic simulation of parallel execution. DetHashSet uses
a deterministic hasher, ensuring that scheduling decisions are
reproducible across runs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
perf(scheduler): optimize local queue push and priority batch draining

Three scheduler-level optimizations:

- local_queue.rs: Remove O(N) contains() check from schedule_local_push.
  The previous implementation locked the arena and scanned the queue for
  duplicates on every push. Since the scheduler execution loop naturally
  handles duplicate TaskId polls (a second poll of an already-completed
  task is a no-op), the uniqueness invariant is not required for
  correctness. This eliminates a significant hot-path cost under high
  task churn.

- priority.rs: Cap scratch buffer growth during batch draining. When
  draining tasks of the same priority or deadline, the loop now also
  breaks when scratch.len() >= scratch.capacity(), preventing unbounded
  Vec growth during bulk operations while still batching effectively
  within the pre-allocated capacity.

- Cargo.toml: Add crossbeam-deque 0.8.6 dependency for work-stealing
  scheduler infrastructure.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
test(pool): expand deterministic pool tests with VirtualClock

Significantly improve pool test coverage by introducing VirtualClock-
based deterministic testing infrastructure:

- Add test_cx_with_timer helper that creates a test context with a
  controllable virtual clock, enabling precise time advancement in
  pool lifecycle tests.
- Add hold duration tests for both resource return and discard paths,
  verifying that the pool correctly tracks how long resources were
  checked out using virtual time.
- Add acquire timeout test using VirtualClock to deterministically
  verify that pool acquisition times out after the configured duration
  without relying on wall-clock sleeps.
- Warmup test now uses Duration::from_hours for clearer intent.

These tests replace flaky wall-clock-dependent assertions with
deterministic virtual time, making the pool test suite reliable
under CI load and fast to execute.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
feat(transport): add cooperative yielding budgets to sink and stream

Prevent always-ready transports from starving other tasks by adding
per-poll item budgets that yield back to the scheduler.

- sink.rs: SEND_ALL_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET (1024) for the send_all future.
  When a sink is always ready to accept items, the budget ensures the
  executor regains control after 1024 sends per poll, waking the task
  for continuation on the next scheduler tick.
- stream.rs: FILTER_REJECTION_BUDGET (64) for SymbolStream's filter
  path. When the filter rejects many consecutive items from an always-
  ready underlying stream, the budget prevents unbounded spinning.
  Includes AlwaysReadyRejectingStream test confirming the budget is
  respected and the stream eventually completes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: asupersync
repository.description: Async runtime for Rust where correctness is structural: region-owned tasks, cancel-correct protocols, capability-gated effects, and deterministic replay testing

commit.message:
feat(codec): add cooperative read budgets to Framed and FramedRead

Prevent always-ready byte sources from monopolizing the executor by
capping the number of decode passes per poll_next invocation.

- framed.rs: MAX_READ_PASSES_PER_POLL (32) limits how many times the
  inner codec's decode() is called in a single poll. When the budget
  is exhausted, the waker is notified and Poll::Pending is returned,
  allowing other tasks to make progress. Includes AlwaysReadyDuplex
  test that verifies partial draining and eventual completion across
  multiple polls.
- framed_read.rs: Same MAX_READ_PASSES_PER_POLL (32) pattern applied
  to the read-only FramedRead. Includes AlwaysReadyByteReader test
  confirming cooperative behavior with a never-empty byte source.

This complements the stream-level cooperative budgets by applying the
same fairness principle at the codec layer, where a fast producer
could otherwise starve sibling tasks by continuously filling the read
buffer within a single poll.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(stream): add cooperative yielding budgets to chunks, forward, try_stream combinators

Prevent always-ready streams from monopolizing the executor by adding
per-poll item budgets that yield back to the scheduler after processing
a bounded number of items.

- chunks.rs: CHUNKS_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET (1024) for both Chunks and
  ReadyChunks poll_next implementations. Includes AlwaysReadyCounter
  tests verifying the budget is respected and the stream eventually
  completes across multiple polls.
- forward.rs: FORWARD_SEND_BUDGET (1024) in the async forward()
  function, inserting yield_now() after every budget-worth of items
  to prevent starvation of other tasks sharing the executor.
- try_stream.rs: TRY_STREAM_COOPERATIVE_BUDGET (1024) applied to
  TryCollect, TryFold, and TryForEach — the try-variant combinators
  that previously had unbounded per-poll loops.
- collect.rs: Removed redundant .clone() in test assertion (minor).

This is part of the broader cooperative yielding initiative across all
stream combinators to ensure fair scheduling under high-throughput
always-ready stream workloads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: compliance score now includes Linux/NixOS incidents in total basket

Score was 99% because it only counted Windows compliance bundles, ignoring
370+ active Linux/NixOS incidents. Now queries active incidents from ALL
platforms and treats each as a failure in its category. Expanded category
map covers 60+ check types across Windows, Linux, and NixOS.

Scoring: (passes + 0.5*warnings) / (passes + warnings + fails + incidents)
Applied to both admin and client portal compliance-health endpoints.

commit.message:
feat(http,runtime): add stale connection retry, idle cleanup, and reactor self-wake on registration

Improve HTTP client reliability by automatically retrying safe requests
when a pooled connection turns out to be stale, proactively cleaning up
expired idle connections, and ensuring the I/O driver wakes the reactor
when new sources are registered during a blocking poll.

HTTP client (http_client.rs):
- Detect stale pooled connection failures (BrokenPipe, ConnectionReset,
  ConnectionAborted, NotConnected, UnexpectedEof) on reused connections
- Automatically retry GET/HEAD/OPTIONS/TRACE on a fresh connection when
  a reused connection fails with a stale-reuse error
- Add cleanup_expired_idle_connections() to proactively evict expired
  idle connections and their associated IO handles
- Call cleanup on every connection acquisition to prevent stale IO buildup
- Add tests for idle connection cleanup and stale-reuse retry behavior

Connection pool (pool.rs):
- Add cleanup_expired_entries() returning removed (PoolKey, id) pairs
  so callers can also clean up associated IO handles
- Keep cleanup_expired() as a backwards-compatible count-only wrapper
- Add get_connection_meta() for looking up individual connection state
- Add test verifying expired entry removal returns correct IDs

I/O driver (io_driver.rs):
- IoRegistration now holds Arc<dyn Reactor> and Arc<AtomicBool> for
  self-wake capability
- Add wake_polling_reactor() to interrupt blocked reactor poll when
  registering or re-arming sources from another thread
- Wake reactor on new source registration if currently polling

io_uring reactor:
- Additional refinements to poll rearm and state management

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: debounce cooperative budget, cx fast_cancel sync, benchmark iter_batched, beads sync

Four changes from concurrent agents:

- Debounce (src/stream/debounce.rs): Add DEBOUNCE_READY_DRAIN_BUDGET (1024)
  cooperative yielding, matching the pattern established in the other stream
  consumers. Prevents always-ready upstream from monopolizing executor while
  replacing the buffered item in a tight loop. Includes TrackWaker,
  AlwaysReadyCounter test helpers and
  debounce_yields_cooperatively_on_always_ready_burst test.

- Cx (src/cx/cx.rs): Add missing fast_cancel.store() in
  set_cancel_internal() so the fast-path atomic flag stays in sync with
  the guarded cancel_requested field. Without this, the fast-path check
  in poll could miss a cancellation set via set_cancel_internal.

- Benchmarks (benches/tracing_overhead.rs): Switch from hoisting state
  creation outside the iter loop to using iter_batched with
  SmallInput, which is the idiomatic Criterion approach for benchmarks
  that need fresh state per iteration without measuring setup.

- Beads issue tracker: Updated with latest issue.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
